benchmarks.name: kenki-posi dataset
benchmarks.summary: Headline-only. The paper (arXiv:2101.06563) states it evaluates Absolute Trajectory RMSE against ground truth from RTK-GNSS on the authors' construction-site sequences and compares against state-of-the-art dynamic visual SLAM methods (e.g. DynaSLAM, ORB-SLAM2), claiming 'competitive result trajectory accuracy'. The quantitative results table sits in the experimental section of the PDF, which the reader returned truncated (only up to Section 3.2); specific ATE RMSE numbers could not be verified from the accessible text, so no metric values are recorded.
benchmarks.comparison_scope: Results sourced from the original publication and associated benchmark.
benchmarks.protocol: Metrics reported as in the source publication.
benchmarks.submission_policy: Open: results compiled from published papers and public leaderboards.

benchmarks.name: DrafterBench: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Tasks Automation in Civil Engineering
benchmarks.summary: Headline-only. The paper reports Table 2 comparing six LLMs (OpenAI o1, GPT-4o-2024-08-06, Claude-3.5-Sonnet-20241022, DeepSeek-v3, Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct, Llama-3-70B-Instruct) on 12 task types and a comprehensive score (0-100). However Table 2 is rendered as an image in the HTML and its cell values could not be extracted; only the qualitative ranking in the text is verifiable (OpenAI o1 first; Claude-3.5-Sonnet and DeepSeek-v3-685B neck-and-neck second tier; o1 still ~20 points short of 100). No per-model numeric scores are recorded.

benchmarks.name: HangCon Dataset: Hanging Objects in Construction Sites
benchmarks.dataset_name: HangCon Dataset: Hanging Objects in Construction Sites
benchmarks.summary: Headline-only result for the HangCon dataset. The ASCE paper is paywalled; only the abstract is accessible, which reports aggregate best mAP across 10 benchmark models (Faster R-CNN, RetinaNet, Cascade R-CNN, CornerNet, CenterNet, DETR, ATSS, EfficientDet, Deformable-DETR, YOLOv5, YOLOv8). The GitHub repo lists the 10 models but gives no per-model mAP. Best mAP 71.63% for hanging objects alone, improving to 76.01% with unified annotations of objects+ropes.

benchmarks.comparison_scope: Results sourced from the original publication and associated benchmark.
benchmarks.protocol: Metrics reported as in the source publication.
benchmarks.submission_policy: Open: results compiled from published papers and public leaderboards.

benchmarks.name: BuildingWorld Dataset
benchmarks.summary: Reported benchmark results for BuildingWorld from the source paper. Table 2 reports PBWR (Huang et al. 2024) trained on BuildingWorld-derived simulated data vs. real data, evaluated on the Building3D test set, following Building3D metrics (ACO, corner/edge precision-recall and F1).

results.method: PBWR* (trained on simulated, eval on simulated)
PBWR (trained on real, eval on real)
PBWR* (trained on simulated, eval on real)

benchmarks.name: ConstScene Dataset

benchmarks.name: GOOSE-Ex Dataset
benchmarks.task: Semantic segmentation (2D image and 3D point cloud)
benchmarks.summary: Reported benchmark results for GOOSE-Ex from the source paper (Table II). State-of-the-art 2D and 3D segmentation models trained on GOOSE and fine-tuned on GOOSE-Ex, evaluated on the GOOSE-Ex test set. mIoU is reported for both fine-grained class labels (class) and aggregated category labels (category).
benchmarks.comparison_scope: Results sourced from the original publication and associated benchmark.
benchmarks.protocol: Metrics reported as in the source publication.
benchmarks.submission_policy: Open: results compiled from published papers and public leaderboards.

benchmarks.name: Rail3D: Multi-Context Point Cloud Dataset for Railway Semantic Segmentation
benchmarks.dataset_name: Rail3D: Multi-Context Point Cloud Dataset for Railway Semantic Segmentation
benchmarks.summary: Reported benchmark results for Rail3D from the source paper. Rail3D comprises three railway contexts: HMLS (Hungary), SNCF (France), and INFRABEL (Belgium). Three baseline models (KPConv, 3DMASC/Random Forest, LightGBM) were evaluated on each subset using Overall Accuracy (OA) and mean Intersection over Union (mIoU).

benchmarks.name: TOPO-Loss-Crack: continuity-preserving crack detection dataset
benchmarks.summary: Reported benchmark results for the TOPO-Loss-Crack (Wild) dataset from the source paper, Table 3. All six rows are the same TernausNet architecture trained with different loss functions.
benchmarks.source_label: Pantoja-Rosero et al. 2022, Constr. Build. Mater. 344:128264 (CC BY 4.0, ETH Zurich)
benchmarks.notes: F1-skeleton is skeleton-tolerant F1 (20px margin), percent. CPP = Cracks Per Patch continuity error, lower better. NOTE: Expe-dataset columns (F1-skeleton_expe, cpp_expe) omitted here for the Wild-only board; values available in source Table 3.

benchmarks.protocol: Metrics reported as in the source publication.
